definitions: A method of rendering computer graphics where the image is divided into smaller rectangular sections (tiles) that are rendered independently and then combined to form the final image.
A technique used in graphics processing units (GPUs) to optimize rendering performance by processing small portions of a scene at a time, often to reduce memory bandwidth.
examples: Modern GPUs often employ tiled rendering.
The game engine utilizes tiled rendering for efficiency.
Tiled rendering improves performance on mobile devices.
